title: Ibuprofen
content_markdown:
##### Type

Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory

##### Presentations

200mg tablet
 100mg/5ml liquid 

##### Actions

- Analgesic

- Anti-pyretic

- Anti-inflammatory

- Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis via inhibition of COX-1 and COX-2

#### Uses

Analgesia for mild-moderate pain due to inflammation

#### Adverse Effects

- Nausea

- Dyspepsia 

- GI ulceration or bleeding

- Diarrhoea

- Headache

- Dizziness

#### Contraindications

- Known hypersensitivity to NSAIDs

- Acute asthma

- Dehydration

- Current peptic ulcers

- Known or suspected active bleeding

- Inability to swallow/impaired gag reflex

- End stage renal or hepatic failure

- Acute heart failure

- Previous ibuprofen administration within 4 hours

#### Precautions

- History of peptic ulcers or GI bleeding

- Hepatic impairment

- Asthma

- Cardiovascular disease

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- **Contraindicated**

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

##### Adult

- ≥40kg: 400mg
- ≤40kg: 200mg

##### Paediatric

- Tablets: 20-40kg: 200mg
- PO (liquid): Ibuprofen 100mg/5ml 
- 3-6 months (6-8 kg): 3 - 4ml
- 6-12 months (8-10 kg): 4 - 5ml- 1-3 years (10-14 kg): 5 - 7ml- 3-5 years (14-18 kg): 7 - 9ml- 5-7 years (18-22 kg): 9 - 11ml- 7-9 years (22-28 kg): 11 - 14ml- 9-12 years (28-40 kg): 14 - 20ml
